baseball.author: Lucas
baseball.title: Today's Board:
baseball.body:
LEAVING THX
Jackson Chourio (MIL) vs. STL (RHP Matt Svanson)
Odds: +500 to homer
Chourio's white hot - .292 average, .524 slug, 13 homers in 55 games (deep in 20% of his games this year). Today he draws Matt Svanson, a 2-1 arm with a shaky 4.67 ERA - not exactly lockdown stuff. Only wrinkle: Chourio went 0-for-4 last time he saw the Cardinals. But a shaky ERA arm plus a red-hot bat says the yard's calling his name.

MORTGAGE THE HOUSE
Luis Garcia Jr. (WSH) vs. HOU (RHP Tatsuya Imai)
Odds: +540 to homer
Garcia's an absolute menace right now - 18-19 homers on the year, carrying the Nationals offense outright with 9 homers and a 1.394 OPS over his last 15 games. Imai is turrible.

100% VIBES
Colson Montgomery (CWS) vs. BOS
Odds: +330 to homer
No overthinking this one - Montgomery's in the middle of a legit power surge, 23 homers already, going deep in nearly 26% of his games this year. "Big Smooth" is just seeing it right now. Pure gut call.

LOTTO TICKET
Vladimir Guerrero Jr. (TOR) vs. SF
Odds: +750 to homer
He's Vlad Guerrero. He should consider hitting a home run.

SICKO PICK
Jordan Walker (STL) vs. MIL (RHP Jacob Misiorowski)
Odds: +560 to homer
Misiorowski is filthy - 1.47 ERA, 0.78 WHIP, hit 104.5 mph this year (an MLB record for a starter). Walker's the real deal too: .292 average, 20 bombs, top-20 OPS in the league. Betting a dinger off one of the nastiest arms in baseball isn't smart, it's sick. That's the point.

RETIREMENT LAY (buy a log cabin in Montana and never be heard from again)
Parlay: Luis Garcia Jr. (+540) + Colson Montgomery (+330) + Jackson Chourio (+500)
Combined odds: ~+16,412
Three of the hottest bats on the slate, stacked into one. Hit all three and it's not a bet, it's a life event - pack the truck, Montana's calling.

soccer.author: Liam
soccer.title: Switzerland vs Colombia - Match Prediction
soccer.body:
The next and final game for today is Switzerland v Colombia. Unlike the other game, I think this will be a close battle. The Swiss have a very strong side led by their captain for many years, Granit Xhaka. I believe the Swiss will have many chances but not have much of the ball in this game - the Colombian side just has too many attacking weapons and are flat out more talented, with Luis Diaz having a fantastic tournament so far. This will be a close battle and a great watch if you love defensive battles with a moment of brilliance. In this case I believe Colombia will provide that moment.

Prediction
Colombia 1
Switzerland 0

Bets
Safe bets: Colombia to advance, Under 3.5 goals

Risky bets: Over 2.5 goals, Draw 90 minutes

Hot bets: Diaz anytime goal scorer, Embolo 2+ shots on target

opinions.author: Cade
opinions.title: USMNT Embarasses America
opinions.body:
You can't win at everything.

It's a simple fact of life. No civilization to exist in recorded human history has been able to accomplish such a feat. Last night, America had a come to Jesus realization. We fucking suck at soccer.

We were dooped again in the every 4 year World Cup soccer fandom cycle. Maybe it's just on us at this point. How could we be so stupid as to believe that wins against Paraguay, Australia, and Boznia-Herzosomething were a true indicator of how good this band of Z-tier American athletes and castaways from foreign countries were?

The "men" that represented us last night should simply be ashamed. As a red blooded American Patriot, i think a fair compromise would be for Christian Pulisic to immediately relinquish citizenship to the United States of America. I don't think he should ever be allowed to step foot in the 48 contiguous ever again (potentially open to him owning property in Alaska or Hawaii should he agree). I read somewhere that he has Croatian citizenship as well. Christian, America would love for you to switch and represent them.

The American soccer propaganda machine was up to their usual tricks after last nights curb stomping. "They won over the hearts of Americans" or "The future is bright, we just aren't there yet". We will never get there.

It's just that simple. We don't care about that sport in this country. Think anyone in Belgium can hit a 100mph fastball? Or bring down Derrick Henry as he breaks into the second level? The answer is absolutely fucking not. Kids in Belgium grow up stuffing their fucking faces with waffles, chocolates and dreaming of the World Cup. We want to score the winning touchdown in the Super Bowl.

If the President is going to create an International incident so that a Nigerian-Brit can play for the U.S. in a soccer game, you better be able to back it up. Last night is a clear indication that America will never be able to back it up. Come 2030 let's save everyone some time. Don't bother putting a team on the field for a sport we don't care about.
